Title: RE: FW: Your archive mailbox is almost full. [ thread::DJb_HWWqIZGR2bAu7ybYHAc:: ]
Notes:
As per the email instructions, we accessed the Microsoft 365 portal, verified the details, and shared the below update with Colleen.


Hi Colleen, 

We have checked and noticed that Sarah’s archive mailbox is approaching its storage limit, with 90.15 GB used out of the available 100 GB. As a result, Sarah has received a warning indicating that the archive storage is nearing capacity.

Currently, with the Office 365 E3 license, the archive mailbox has a maximum storage limit of 100 GB. To continue archiving emails beyond this limit, we recommend upgrading to the Office 365 E5 plan. This upgrade includes auto-expanding archiving, which allows the archive mailbox to automatically grow beyond 100 GB and helps avoid any disruption to the archiving process.

Benefits of upgrading to Office 365 E5 include:

  • Auto-expanding archiving, enabling the archive mailbox to grow without manual intervention.
  • Enhanced security features and advanced compliance tools included with the E5 plan.

Alternatively, if you prefer, you can manage the archive storage manually by deleting unnecessary items or moving older emails to free up space.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. Please let us know how you would like to proceed, and feel free to reach out if you have any questions

Title: Re: Can’t join list - Microsoft To Do
Notes:
Received confirmation from Colleen to proceed with adding the remaining parties to the Microsoft To Do distribution list. I created the distribution list and successfully added the external member bayfamily04@gmail.com as per Colleen’s request.

However, after testing the distribution list by sending a test email, it was found that the email was not delivered to the external member. Despite multiple attempts, the issue persisted.

Given that the issue requires further troubleshooting, I handed over the case to the next shift for additional investigation and resolution.

Status: Handover to next shift for further troubleshooting.

Title: Re: Can’t join list - Microsoft To Do
Notes: Completed troubleshooting for the distribution list issue by running a detailed message trace, which showed previous delivery failures caused by an incorrect SMTP domain. Verified the email address configuration and confirmed the DL had an incorrect SMTP entry. Recreated the distribution list with the correct address (thestrandhoa@vintagegroupre.com), updated the SMTP settings, and ensured all configurations were properly applied. Added the test user for validation, sent test emails, and confirmed successful delivery through message trace. Verified that the DL is now expanding correctly and mailflow is functioning as expected. Issue resolved.

Title: This is what I get
Notes: Reviewed the issue reported by the user where Renee was unable to open the Microsoft To Do list despite successfully receiving emails from the distribution list. Identified that the error message she encountered was related to Microsoft attempting to create a passkey during account setup, which is a device/browser-specific prompt and not related to the DL or the To Do link itself. Advised that Renee does not need a passkey to access Microsoft To Do and instructed her to bypass the passkey setup by selecting Cancel and choosing Use password instead or Skip. Also recommended signing in via https://account.microsoft.com/ using Chrome or a computer to complete the Microsoft account setup for her Gmail address without creating a passkey. Explained that once she signs in successfully, she should be able to open the shared Microsoft To Do list without issues.

Title: SharePoint- Check Request Form
Notes: Reviewed SharePoint/Microsoft Forms routing for the Check Request form. Identified that email notifications are not handled by SharePoint or Forms directly but through a Power Automate flow owned by the ThinkAI Admin service account. Verified that current permissions do not allow modification of notification recipients. Informed user that only the ThinkAI Admin account can update the routing. Contacted Manisha Verma regarding access to the ThinkAI Admin account; she confirmed she will review but cannot address immediately and expects an update by Monday/Tuesday. Awaiting her confirmation to proceed.

Title: Vintage Group - Monday 2.16 Closed
Notes:
Ticket Summary – 02/16/2026 (President’s Day)

As assigned, due to the holiday (President’s Day – 02/16/2026), the office had a recorded holiday message in place.

Downloaded the recorded message initially and then re-downloaded it again after an hour to ensure the latest version was captured. Merged the recordings and converted the final file to meet 3CX requirements (mono/single channel, 8kHz compression format).

Uploaded and applied the converted file to the main line.
Tested inbound call flow — confirmed the message is playing correctly and functioning as expected.

Title: Vintage Group - Monday 2.16 Closed
Notes:
Issue:
During the President's Day holiday, we set up a custom call recording with a voicemail greeting for the main line, informing callers that the office was closed. Now that the holiday is over, we need to revert the settings to their normal operation.

Current Status:
The holiday-specific settings have been fully reverted, and the main line is now operating as usual, with the correct voicemail greeting and call routing restored.
